catalyst.source: Donor-acceptor two-dimensional covalent organic frameworks PD-COF-23 and PD-COF-23-Ni are constructed, and applied for selective CO 2 redution with 20.9 mol g -1 h -1 and 40.0 mol g -1 h -1 CO conversion, respectively, in the absence of any additional photosensitizers and noble metal co-catalytst within an operation time of 25 h.
catalyst.context: Donor-acceptor two-dimensional covalent organic frameworks PD-COF-23 and PD-COF-23-Ni are constructed, and applied for selective CO 2 redution with 20.9 mol g -1 h -1 and 40.0 mol g -1 h -1 CO conversion, respectively, in the absence of any additional photosensitizers and noble metal co-catalytst within an operation time of 25 h.The mutlilayer nanosheet structure, efficient charge separation and transport, and internal reductive quenching cycle of NiTAPP fragments for PD-COF-23-Ni lead to its higher photocatalytic efficiency than that of PD-COF-23.

catalyst.source: Donor-acceptor two-dimensional covalent organic frameworks PD-COF-23 and PD-COF-23-Ni are constructed, and applied for selective CO 2 redution with 20.9 mol g -1 h -1 and 40.0 mol g -1 h -1 CO conversion, respectively, in the absence of any additional photosensitizers and noble metal co-catalytst within an operation time of 25 h.
catalyst.context: Donor-acceptor two-dimensional covalent organic frameworks PD-COF-23 and PD-COF-23-Ni are constructed, and applied for selective CO 2 redution with 20.9 mol g -1 h -1 and 40.0 mol g -1 h -1 CO conversion, respectively, in the absence of any additional photosensitizers and noble metal co-catalytst within an operation time of 25 h.The mutlilayer nanosheet structure, efficient charge separation and transport, and internal reductive quenching cycle of NiTAPP fragments for PD-COF-23-Ni lead to its higher photocatalytic efficiency than that of PD-COF-23.

catalyst.source: In summary, donor-acceptor 2D COFs of PD-COF-23 and PDCOF-23-Ni were successfully developed for visible light photocatalytic CO 2 reduction to CO with high selectivity.
catalyst.context: In summary, donor-acceptor 2D COFs of PD-COF-23 and PDCOF-23-Ni were successfully developed for visible light photocatalytic CO 2 reduction to CO with high selectivity.Typically, PD-COF-23-Ni showed higher CO production efficiency of 40.0 mol g -1 h -1 than PD-COF-23 of 20.9 mol g -1 h -1 within an operation time of 25 h.Due to the donoracceptor structure configuration of the two COFs, the electron-hole pairs upon visible light irradiation were separated and transported from electron-donating TAPP or NiTAPP to electron deficient DPP for CO 2 reduction.On the other hand, NiTAPP can also reduce CO 2 reduction to CO at the Ni(II) centers through an internal reductive quenching cycle, in which NiTAPP acts as both photosensitizer and catalytic center. This work was supported by Hong Kong Baptist University (FRG2-17-18-068, RC-ICRS15-1602E, RC-ICRS161702C-CHE, RC-ICRS-18-19-01A, RC-IRMS161702CHEM). Scheme 1. Synthetic route for PD-COF-23 and PD-COF-23-Ni. yield of CO (mol), N A 6.02 10 -23 mol -1 , I the intensity of irradiation light (5.54 10 -3 W cm 2 ), A irradiation area (3.14 cm 2 ) t photoreaction time (3600 s), the wavelength of the monochromatic light (420 10 -9 m) Fig.1 Experimental and simulated XRD spectra of PD-COF-23 (a) and PD-COF-23 -Ni (b) Fig. 2 SEM of PD-COF-23 (left) and PD-COF-23-Ni (right) Photocurrent (Acm 2 ) Fig. 4 (a) Frontier orbitals from DFT calculation; (b) The proposed photocatalytic mechanism of PD-COF-23-Ni. There are no conflicts to declare. Two 2-D donor-acceptor covalent organic frameworks built from porphyrin and diketopyrrolopyrrole are capable of efficiently and selectively reducing CO 2 into CO, specifically, without any additional photosensitizers or co-catalysts. Published on 16 October 2020.Downloaded by University of New England on 10162020 7:04:20 AM. View Article Online DOI: 10.1039D0DT03205K

light source.source: The light source was an LED monochromatic point lamp (3 W, 420 nm).
light source.context: In a pyrex tube, 5,10,15,20-tetrakis(4-aminophenyl)porphyrin (TAPP) (6.7 mg, 0.01 mmol) and DPP-CHO (9.1 mg, 0.02 mmol) was dissolved in a mixture of 3 mL of n-butanol and 1 mL of mesitylene.Then, 0.1 ml of aqueous acetic (6 M) was added. The reaction mixture was sonicated for five minutes to get homogeneous solution.The reaction vessel was degassed by three freeze-pump-thaw a screw cap and heated at 120 C for five days.The obtained dark precipitate was collected by filtration and washed with THF and acetone for three times.The powder obtained was exchanged by acetone with a Soxhlet extraction apparatus and subsequently dried under vacuum for 12 h to get PD-COF-23 as dark powder (9.4 mg, 52). The synthesis of PD-COF-23-Ni was processed similarly to PD-COF-23 using NiTAPP instead of TAPP, affording PD-COF-23-Ni as red powder (6.2 mg, 34). The photo-electrochemical analysis was carried out in a conventional three electrode cell system, using Pt plates as counter electrodes and a AgAgClsaturated KCl (aq) electrode as reference electrode.The working electrode was prepared on fluorine-doped tin oxide (ITO, 2 cm 2 cm) glass, cleaned by sonication in acetone and ethanol for 30 min, and then dried.The ITO slide was dip-coated with 0.55 mL of slurry, which was obtained from the mixture of photocatalyst (1 mg), ethanol (0.5 mL) and Nafion solution (0.05 mL, 5 ww in water and 1propanol) under sonication for 30 min.The photocurrent measurements were conducted with a CHI660C workstation.The light source was an LED monochromatic point lamp (3 W, 420 nm).The light spot effective area on the working electrode was set as 2cm 2 and 0.5 M Na 2 SO 4 aqueous solution was used as the electrolyte.The open circuit voltages were set as the initial bias voltages in the transient photocurrent responses tests. The experiments were performed in a sealed quartz bottle containing 2.5 mg of catalyst dispersed in 3 mL of acetonitrile and 2 mL of water with 0.5 mL of TEOA as a sacrificial regent.Then, the mixture was purged with CO 2 for 10 min and then sealed at 1 atm CO 2 .A 300 W Xe lamp (Zhong Jiao Jin Yuan, Model CEL-HXF300) was used as the light source.After irradiation, the released gas (400 L) was collected by syringe from the headspace of the reactor and analyzed by gas chromatography (Shimadzu, GC-2014, Japan, with ultrapure Ar as a carrier gas) equipped with a TDX-01(5 A molecular sieve column) and a flame ionization detector (FID). The apparent quantum efficiency (AQE) for CO evolution was measured using monochromatic visible light (420 nm).And the AQE was calculated as follow equation:

light source.source: yield of CO (mol), N A 6.02 10 -23 mol -1 , I the intensity of irradiation light (5.54 10 -3 W cm 2 ), A irradiation area (3.14 cm 2 ) t photoreaction time (3600 s), the wavelength of the monochromatic light (420 10 -9 m)

This work was supported by Hong Kong Baptist University (FRG2-17-18-068, RC-ICRS15-1602E, RC-ICRS161702C-CHE, RC-ICRS-18-19-01A, RC-IRMS161702CHEM). Scheme 1. Synthetic route for PD-COF-23 and PD-COF-23-Ni. yield of CO (mol), N A 6.02 10 -23 mol -1 , I the intensity of irradiation light (5.54 10 -3 W cm 2 ), A irradiation area (3.14 cm 2 ) t photoreaction time (3600 s), the wavelength of the monochromatic light (420 10 -9 m) Fig.1 Experimental and simulated XRD spectra of PD-COF-23 (a) and PD-COF-23 -Ni (b) Fig. 2 SEM of PD-COF-23 (left) and PD-COF-23-Ni (right) Photocurrent (Acm 2 ) Fig. 4 (a) Frontier orbitals from DFT calculation; (b) The proposed photocatalytic mechanism of PD-COF-23-Ni. There are no conflicts to declare. Two 2-D donor-acceptor covalent organic frameworks built from porphyrin and diketopyrrolopyrrole are capable of efficiently and selectively reducing CO 2 into CO, specifically, without any additional photosensitizers or co-catalysts.
